title: Buttery Peruvian Jjante
description: A Peruvian dish featuring almond flour, binchotan-charcoal, and a touch of butter to create a pillowy and energizing meal.

ingredients.name: Almond flour
ingredients.notes: Make sure to use freshly ground almond flour for the best results.

ingredients.name: Binchotan charcoal
ingredients.notes: You can find binchotan charcoal at most Asian grocery stores.

ingredients.name: Butter
ingredients.notes: Unsalted butter works best for this recipe.

ingredients.name: Diced sweet potato
ingredients.notes: Use any variety of sweet potato you like.

ingredients.name: Diced onion
ingredients.notes: Red onion adds a nice sweetness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic before adding it to the mixture.

ingredients.name: Egg
ingredients.notes: Make sure to beat the egg before adding it to the mixture.

ingredients.name: Paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a nice smokiness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Salt
ingredients.notes: Use kosher salt for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Black pepper
ingredients.notes: Freshly ground black pepper is always best.

ingredients.name: Chopped fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: Cilantro adds a nice freshness to the dish.

ingredients.name: Lime juice
ingredients.notes: Freshly squeezed lime juice is always best.

ingredients.name: Sour cream
ingredients.notes: Optional, but adds a nice tanginess to the dish.

instructions: In a large bowl, combine the almond flour, binchotan charcoal, and a pinch of salt. Mix well until the charcoal is evenly distributed.
Add the diced sweet potato, onion, garlic, egg, paprika, salt, and black pepper to the bowl. Mix until everything is well combined.
Using your hands, shape the mixture into small patties, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter and 1/2 inch thick.
Heat a large non-stick skillet or griddle over medium heat. Add 2 tablespoons of butter to the skillet and let it melt.
Add 4-5 patties to the skillet and cook for 3-4 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown and cooked through.
Serve the Jjante hot with a dollop of sour cream and a sprinkle of chopped cilantro, if desired.
Garnish with a squeeze of lime juice and a sprinkle of paprika, if desired.
